body.title: [gated] Containers/sandbox device — full-isolation substrate for builder code execution
body.description:
**GATED — design-first; do not sprint until Akien reopens. Filed 2026-07-07 from D-containers-sandbox-device-2026-07-07 (raised while sprinting T-aider-real-uu-ticket-proof).**

**Problem / shape:** The aider builder runs qwen-generated code + its tests in a throwaway clone, but in the HOST process/machine/DB context. T-aider-real-uu-ticket-proof solves clone-test IMPORT isolation with a lightweight finder-disable (neutralize setuptools' editable MetaPathFinder in the gate's test subprocess). That fix isolates imports only — not filesystem, network, or Postgres side-effects, and does not sandbox untrusted code execution. As the swarm scales to ~8 builders across 4 boxes, running generated code with ambient host/DB access is a CP6 exposure.

**Decision:** Build a `containers` (sandbox) device (device.py/shim.py, independently deployable) that runs builder work in a container with a fresh FS, fresh Python env (clone is the only unseen_university — finder problem structurally absent), and no ambient DB/network/FS access. Route the aider gate's test-run through it. Supersedes the finder-disable hack.

**Open constraints (design must resolve):** (1) WINDOWS portability — the swarm has a Windows box; Docker Desktop is heavier + a new dep across 4 laptops; decide runtime (docker/podman/other) and whether every box needs it. (2) COLD-START — container spin-up is seconds/run; the finder-disable may remain the hot path for cheap tickets, containers reserved for untrusted/heavy runs. (3) the container must still reach Hex/Ollama for inference while blocked from the host's real DB.

**Completion test (at gate-open):** an aider gate test-run executes inside the container against a full UU clone (clone-only install, no editable finder), AND a hostile probe that attempts to reach the real Postgres is contained — where the finder-disable path would not contain it.

**Affected files:** TBD at design (new unseen_university/devices/containers/; aider runner._run_tests routing). No code while gated.

**Design rules:** proof-on-close; device.py/shim.py design center; no SQLite; Windows-portable paths; supersede-not-duplicate the finder-disable.

**Test plan:** the containment + correct-clone-result test above.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

ABSORBED into T-tester-rackmount (D-dev-pipeline-stations-2026-07-07, 2026-07-07): the sandbox container is now the TESTER station's execution environment, not a standalone device (one owner, avoids torn architecture). Build it as part of the tester rackmount. This ticket stays as the container-capability spec; do not build a co-equal containers device.
body.gate: design-first; Akien reopens after offload-loop debt clears
